Transcrição Completa: 10 I'm Traveling This Week Let's Move Forward Next Week
[00:00] I'm traveling this week. Let's move forward next week instead.
[00:05] I want to be clear. I don't really think that's an objection.
[00:09] Certain instances, we can overcome it though by saying that there's like a certain amount of work that we need to do upfront that we don't really need the person there for and we could already have the deal advanced by the time they get back.
[00:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Hands-On vs Hands-Off Client Requirements"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Comparação entre processos que requerem participação ativa do cliente vs processos automatizados, timeline de trabalho durante viagem
[00:24] In other instances, you might need the person to do some stuff with you if you're going to close a deal with them.
[00:30] And it just depends on like how hands-on you need the client to be in order to actually get the deal closed. Okay.
[00:37] So, as an example, if you actually need the person to be hands-on, it's better, hey, I'm traveling this week. I'd rather do it next week instead.
[00:45] Great. What day do you get back? Oh, I get back on Tuesday.
[00:50] Awesome. How about I give you a day or two to settle in? Let's get a follow-up call scheduled on Thursday.
[00:57] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Onboarding Call Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Tuesday return, Thursday follow-up call
VISUAL: Calendário mostrando retorno na terça e agendamento na quinta, processo de onboarding estruturado, tempo de adaptação
[00:59] We'll treat it as an onboarding call. That way, we can get everything done on that and we'll get you locked in and move forward on next Thursday.
[01:07] Does that sound fair? Yeah. Great. All right, cool. What time works for you?
[01:12] You know, we want to do that in the instance once again that we need the person to actually do some work with us.
[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "No Client Work Required Scenario"
NÚMEROS: 30 minutes or less for agreement review
VISUAL: Processo automatizado onde cliente não precisa fazer trabalho, acordo sendo revisado rapidamente, timeline acelerada
[01:20] Let's use the opposite example, though. We don't need the person to do any work for us, and they hit us with, uh, hey, I'm traveling this week. I'd rather do it next week instead.
[01:30] You know, client, I just want to disclose to you. Once you actually pull the trigger on this, there's nothing that we really need you to do. We do all the work on our end.
[01:39] Once you just simply say yes, once we get you locked in, sign this agreement, which you know, to be honest, was going to take less than maybe 30 minutes to review. If that.
[01:49] I just need you to give me the go-ahead, we can get all that work done. You come back from your trip, you're already a week ahead.
[01:57] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO
─────────────────────────────
TEXTO: "Post-Travel Advantage Positioning"
NÚMEROS: 1 week ahead advantage
VISUAL: Pessoa retornando de viagem já com progresso feito, comparação entre estar atrasado vs estar adiantado, vantagem competitiva
[01:59] I hate coming back from trips and feeling behind. I don't know about you, but I'd rather get you ahead than just add another task of something to do once you come back from your trip.
[02:09] Does that sound fair for us to get started today instead of next week?
